value.body:
Found during a follow-up static issue scan of the Cycle desktop renderer.

Problem:
`NotificationProvider` limits visible notifications to five by slicing state, but it does not clear timers for notifications that are pruned from state. Those timers remain active until their original duration elapses and can accumulate under bursts of notifications, especially for long-duration notifications.

Evidence:
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/notifications/NotificationProvider.tsx:26-32` defines `maxVisibleNotifications = 5` and stores timers in a `Map`.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/notifications/NotificationProvider.tsx:55-61` prunes older notifications with `current.slice(...)` when adding a new notification.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/notifications/NotificationProvider.tsx:63-70` registers a timeout for each new notification.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/notifications/NotificationProvider.tsx:34-40` can clear an individual timer, but the prune path does not call it for dropped notification ids.

Suggested remediation:
- When pruning notifications, compute the dropped ids and clear their timers immediately.
- Alternatively centralize notification removal so all removal paths clear timers consistently.
- Add a focused test for adding more than five notifications and asserting timers for pruned notifications are cleared.

Acceptance criteria:
- Timers for notifications removed by max-visible pruning are cleared immediately.
- Manual dismiss, clear-all, timeout expiry, and prune paths all use consistent cleanup.
- Burst notification behavior does not leave stale timer entries in the provider map.

frontmatter.title: Clear notification timers when visible notifications are pruned

value.title: Clear notification timers when visible notifications are pruned
